case_name: Yolanda Taylor, Catina Smith, Cynthia Smith, and Vernaon Taylor, Individually and On Behalf of Delores Jean Bogan (D) v. Nexion Health at Pierremont, Inc., D/B/A Pierremont Healthcare Center – Shreveport, University

summary.brief: Yolanda Taylor, Catina Smith, Cynthia Smith, and Vernaon Taylor, Individually and On Behalf of Delores Jean Bogan (D) v. Nexion Health at Pierremont, Inc., D/B/A Pierremont Healthcare Center – Shreveport, University. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: The nursing facility breached the standard of care by sending the patient with the wrong chart, with the result that the hospital was unaware of the woman’s name or patient history for a period of time.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: A jury found the nursing facility liable for $150,000 in damages for the patient’s lost chance of survival.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Jason Reingold, a cardiologist from Atlanta, testified that it was more likely than not that if LSU doctors had known Bogan’s identity and conditions when she arrived at the hospital, they would have had a better chance at saving her life.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Jason Reingold

evidence_index.text: Upon further investigation due to inconsistencies with PE and patient’s chart, appears that NH sent wrong paperwork with patient and given her unresponsiveness was not able to verify identity.
evidence_index.speaker: LSU treating physicians

evidence_index.text: We affirm the trial court’s verdict and damage award.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The jury’s findings of fact are reasonable in light of the entire record.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: Taylor testified that Pierremont called her at about 6 a.m. to tell her that her mother had collapsed.
evidence_index.speaker: Yolanda Taylor

evidence_index.text: Smith testified that she received a phone call from Pierremont around 6 a.m. and drove to LSU.
evidence_index.speaker: Cynthia Smith

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient collapsed at nursing facility and was unresponsive. Previously had chest pain and was advised to undergo left heart catheterization but declined.

focused_exam.neurological: Pupils not reactive, poor brain activity

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Unresponsive patient with potential cardiac issues

requestables.name: Blood Work
requestables.description: Blood work drawn upon arrival at hospital

reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates severe hypoxia and metabolic disturbance
reveal.decision_impact: Requires immediate correction of metabolic issues

reveal.detailed_findings: Imaging performed but findings not detailed in judgment

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ER after collapse at nursing facility

key_events.event: Patient arrives at ER with incorrect chart

timeline_phases.name: Initial Workup
timeline_phases.description: Initial assessment and incorrect treatment based on wrong chart

key_events.event: Blood work and chest x-ray performed

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ER with unresponsiveness and incorrect chart
decision_points.prompt: What should be the immediate next step in management?

options.description: Verify patient identity and obtain correct medical history
options.clinical_reasoning: Correct patient identification is crucial for appropriate treatment

options.description: Proceed with treatment based on available chart
options.clinical_reasoning: Led to treatment based on incorrect data

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant proceeded with treatment based on incorrect chart
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Treatment was based on the chart of an 89-year-old woman

expected_action_court.description: Verify patient identity and obtain correct medical history
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Essential for accurate diagnosis and treatment

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Incorrect patient data led to inappropriate initial treatment
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Patient identity should have been verified immediately

patient_outcome.description: Patient experienced prolonged ischemic time and passed away

key_findings: Breach of duty by sending incorrect chart
Causation established - delay in treatment reduced chance of survival

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to verify patient identity and obtain correct medical history
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Correct identification would have led to timely intervention

factual_timeline.event: Patient arrives at LSU with incorrect chart

tests_performed.result: Imaging performed but findings not detailed in judgment

diagnoses.diagnosis: Prolonged ischemic time due to delayed treatment

allegations.allegation: Failure to verify patient identity and obtain correct medical history

court_findings.finding: Defendant breached duty of care by sending incorrect chart
court_findings.legal_significance: Breach led to delay in treatment and reduced chance of survival
